hints.accusative: While 'nomen' can be accusative, here it is the subject

hints.1st declension: 1st declension nouns end in -a in the nominative
hints.2nd declension: 2nd declension neuter nouns end in -um, not -en

hints.to eat: That would be the infinitive of 'edere'
hints.to go: That would be the infinitive of 'ire'
hints.to have: That would be the infinitive of 'habere'

hints.edo, edere, edi, esum: These are the principal parts of 'edo' (to eat)
hints.sto, stare, steti, statum: These are the principal parts of 'sto' (to stand)
hints.fio, fieri, factus sum, —: These are the principal parts of 'fio' (to become)

hints.genitive: The genitive singular would be 'divitiae' but this is nominative plural (plurale tantum)
hints.vocative: The vocative would be the same as nominative but here it is not an address

hints.2nd declension: 2nd declension nominative plural ends in -i or -a (neuter)

hints.genitive: The genitive feminine singular is 'multae' but here it modifies plural 'divitiae'
hints.dative: The dative feminine singular would be 'multae' but the context is nominative plural
hints.ablative: The ablative feminine plural would be 'multis'

translation: A good name is better than great riches
